claim: Effective prompting combines familiar human communication (clarity, context, examples) with AI-specific techniques (explicit reasoning, formatted output, bounded constraints)
actionable: When crafting prompts, start with clear task overview, provide context about your use case, add format specifications and examples

claim: Being specific about output constraints (format, length, language, style) ensures you get exactly what you need; vague constraints cause misalignment
actionable: Always specify desired format, length, language, and style constraints explicitly; don't assume the model will infer your preferences

claim: Few-shot prompting (providing 2-5 examples) helps when you have a specific style or pattern; cover diverse cases so the model understands the full pattern range
actionable: Provide 2-5 diverse examples when you have a specific output style you want; ensure examples cover different scenarios

claim: Breaking complex tasks into explicit steps helps models follow your process; chain-of-thought prompting is less necessary with modern reasoning models but still useful for alignment
actionable: List out task steps explicitly for complex requests; modern models can self-reason, but guidance helps align their process with yours

claim: Asking the model to think first before acting produces better results; don't ask for thinking after execution, as it won't improve the output
actionable: Prompt models to reason before answering, not after; extended thinking/reasoning happens upfront and improves quality

claim: Specifying role, perspective, and tone guides how the model approaches a task; it significantly changes output style and reasoning
actionable: Define who you want the model to act as (expert, teacher, etc.) and in what tone; this shapes both reasoning and communication style

claim: Effective prompting is iterative; if a response doesn't match your needs, refine by adding specificity, examples, or breaking tasks into steps
actionable: Treat prompting as an experimental process; iterate by adjusting specificity, examples, and task breakdown until output quality matches expectations
